authority: Cayman Islands Department for International Tax Cooperation (DITC)
reporting_deadline: 31 July following the reporting calendar year
submission_method: DITC Portal (AEOI Portal), CRS XML format
excluded_accounts: Certain registered mutual funds with short-term reporting waivers

INVESTMENT_A: Fund units and limited partnership interests
Equity and debt interests in managed funds
INSURANCE: Variable annuity and life insurance cash values

new_individual.procedure: Self-certification required at account opening. DITC provides standardised self-cert forms. Obtain: name, address, TIN(s), jurisdiction(s) of tax residence, date of birth.

pre_existing_individual_lower_value.procedure: Residence address test. Electronic search for foreign indicia. Cayman FIs commonly use third-party due diligence providers.

pre_existing_individual_high_value.procedure: Enhanced review including relationship manager certification and full electronic/paper search.

new_entity.procedure: Self-certification for entity type. For investment funds, classify as Reporting FI or Passive NFFE. Identify controlling persons for Passive NFFEs (typically investors with >25% interest).

pre_existing_entity.procedure: Review registration documents, constitutional documents, AML due diligence files for entity classification.

account: Account number or investor register reference
income_investment: Total distributions and income paid or credited during the year

derived_fields: Reportability determination for each investor/account holder
Fund classification: Reporting FI vs Passive NFFE vs Exempt FI
Controlling person extraction from investor register or constitutional docs
NAV per unit/interest as of 31 December
USD equivalent for all non-USD denominated accounts
Undocumented investor flag and reason code
Aggregate interest linkage across related accounts within same FI group

key_dates.notification_of_aeoi_obligation: 30 April (FIs must notify DITC of reporting obligation by this date)

penalties.failure_to_report: Penalty up to KYD 50,000 per violation
penalties.false_information: Criminal offence under Cayman Tax Information Authority Law

local_notes: Cayman is a major fund domicile - most Reporting FIs are investment funds or fund administrators
DITC Portal registration is mandatory for all Reporting FIs by 30 April each year
Third-party reporting is common: fund administrators typically file on behalf of funds
Cayman has no income tax so TIN concept is handled via registration number
For investment funds: controlling persons are typically investors with >25% interest in the fund
DITC issues updated guidance annually - always check for current guidance before filing
Nil returns are required if no reportable accounts exist
